https://openalex.org/W2607041014.title: Deep Laplacian Pyramid Networks for Fast and Accurate Super-Resolution
https://openalex.org/W2607041014.content:
Deep Laplacian Pyramid Networks for Fast and Accurate Super-Resolution

Convolutional neural networks have recently demonstrated high-quality reconstruction for single-image super-resolution. In this paper, we propose the Laplacian Pyramid Super-Resolution Network (LapSRN) to progressively reconstruct sub-band residuals of high-resolution images. At each pyramid level, our model takes coarse-resolution feature maps as input, predicts high-frequency residuals, and uses transposed convolutions upsampling finer level. Our method does not require bicubic interpolation pre-processing step thus dramatically reduces computational complexity. We train proposed LapSRN with deep supervision using a robust Charbonnier loss function achieve reconstruction. Furthermore, network generates multi-scale predictions in one feed-forward pass through progressive reconstruction, thereby facilitates resource-aware applications. Extensive quantitative qualitative evaluations on benchmark datasets show that algorithm performs favorably against state-of-the-art methods terms speed accuracy.

https://openalex.org/W2963188159.description:
The article introduces PoseCNN, a novel Convolutional Neural Network designed for 6D object pose estimation in cluttered scenes, addressing challenges posed by occlusions and scene complexity. It details the network's ability to accurately estimate 3D translations and rotations using a unique loss function and a large-scale YCB-Video dataset, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in pose estimation with only color images.

https://openalex.org/W1971687303.description:
The article explores how people instinctively evaluate faces across various traits, revealing that these evaluations can significantly influence social outcomes like electoral success and legal decisions. Through behavioral studies and computer modeling, it identifies two key dimensions—valence and dominance—that shape these evaluations, highlighting the role of facial features in signaling trustworthiness and potential threat.

Deep Multi-scale Convolutional Neural Network for Dynamic Scene Deblurring

Non-uniform blind deblurring for general dynamic scenes is a challenging computer vision problem as blurs arise not only from multiple object motions but also camera shake, scene depth variation. To remove these complicated motion blurs, conventional energy optimization based methods rely on simple assumptions such that blur kernel partially uniform or locally linear. Moreover, recent machine learning depend synthetic datasets generated under assumptions. This makes fail to where difficult approximate parameterize (e.g. boundaries). In this work, we propose multi-scale convolutional neural network restores sharp images in an end-to-end manner caused by various sources. Together, present loss function mimics coarse-to-fine approaches. Furthermore, new large-scale dataset provides pairs of realistic blurry image and the corresponding ground truth are obtained high-speed camera. With proposed model trained dataset, demonstrate empirically our method achieves state-of-the-art performance qualitatively, quantitatively.

image analysis, computational imaging, computer science, convolutional neural network, scene interpretation, scene understanding, image restoration, computer vision, machine learning, dynamic scene, data science, deep learning, deblurring, machine vision, digital image processing
https://openalex.org/W2560533888.description:
The article presents a novel deep multi-scale convolutional neural network designed to effectively address the complex problem of non-uniform blind deblurring in dynamic scenes, which is often complicated by multiple object motions and camera shake. By utilizing a new large-scale dataset of realistic blurry images and their corresponding sharp counterparts, the proposed model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in restoring sharp images through an end-to-end learning approach.
